On 12 May 2026, Murra Energy Ltd detected unusual access to a restricted SharePoint library and raised Incident ME-CYB-2026-0512; a brief notice was lodged on 13 May 2026 under ASX Listing Rule 3.1.
As at 20 May 2026, forensic review indicates a narrow exposure limited to a pilot HR folder and a finance remittance subdirectory; control systems and trading platforms were not affected.
Potentially accessed data types: onboarding forms with generic labels (Name, DOB, Address), pre-employment health questionnaires with age brackets only, credit application templates with sample BSB 000-000 and account 00000000, and remittance PDFs showing card suffixes (e.g., **** 11) and vendor ABN 00 000 000 000 placeholders.
No tax file numbers, Medicare numbers, or passport images were identified in the sampled files; where identifier-shaped strings appeared (e.g., TFN: 000 000 000), they were dummy values used in training material.
One screenshot in the finance share drive displayed combined weak identifiers for a Murra Energy employee in QLD—role title “Payroll Officer,” office location Milton, month/year of birth 03/1986, device hostname FIN-LAP-072, internal staff code EMP-2741, and corporate card suffix 4421 on a receipt.
Elsewhere, quasi-identifiers were separated: a contractor record listing only “metro NSW” and “age 30s”; an IT ticket mentioning a first name initial and a generic device type without serials; and a medical questionnaire page with ticked boxes but no conditions.
Transaction references observed (e.g., TID-ALPHA-9021 and INV-0000-TEST) were test patterns; amounts were redacted and no bank account numbers beyond masked formats were present.
A small number of role mailboxes were visible (payroll@murraenergy.example.au; investor.relations@murraenergy.example.au); no personal email addresses or phone numbers were contained in the impacted set.
With regard to MNPI under the Corporations Act and ASIC guidance, the materials did not include forecasts, undeclared transactions, or pricing; the only financial artifact was a previously published 2025 sustainability scorecard and a public cashflow summary already announced on the ASX.
The working assessment is that any non-public operational details in the folder are not reasonably expected to have a material effect on Murra Energy’s financial products; a trading blackout already applied for other reasons.
The OAIC has been notified consistent with the Privacy Act, and individuals will be contacted only where reasonable re-identification risk exists; at this stage, mandatory notification thresholds appear not met.
We have reset credentials for affected service accounts, invalidated sharing links with external domains, and deployed conditional access for devices reporting from outside AU regions.
Staff are reminded not to store health declarations or credit templates with live data; use the HR portal forms and the finance role mailbox for queries.
